DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel A. Serot, Esq., from Van De Poel, Levy & Allen, LLP, 415-425-4834, for defendant and moving party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted.
DEFENDANT DAN LEE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Demurrer to the first and fifth causes of action sustained without leave to amend. No dismissal has been filed, and Plaintiff has asserted no opposition. The demurrer to the second, third, and fourth causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has ten days to amend the second, third, and fourth causes of action of the first amended complaint. The Court declines to take judicial notice of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint and Plaintiff's Opposition to Defendant Dan Lee's Motion to Vacate the Amended Default Judgment and accompanying documentation. Order signed in open court. Judge: James J. McBride; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel A. Serot, Esq., of Van De Poel, Levy & Allen, LLP, Tel. 925.934.6102, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Heather Wolnick, Esq., of Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, Tel. 415.626.7744, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAN LEE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND AMENDED DEFAULT JUDGMENT is Granted. Service on Defendant Lee by way of substitute service on his estranged wife at an address where he no longer lived is not valid under section 415.20(b). Compliance with the statutory procedures for service of process is essential to establish personal jurisdiction and thus, the default judgment is void and must be set aside pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 473(d). Order signed in open Court. Plaintiff's request for a statement of decision is denied. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

RULING: The matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF EDWARD ELMS' Motion For Order Assigning Defendant Dan Lee's Interest In His Profit Sharing Agreement With Phoenix Rabbit, LLC is Off calendar in light of the ruling on Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment.
